content.full_text:
New York Tempor<IfY State 
Commission Oil Regulation of Lobbying 
ALBANY (HTICI': 
i\lfrcd F. Smith Ofr;.:e Buildillg 
Albany. New York 12225 
NEW YOHK OITICE 
gO Centre Street 
New York. New York 10007 
May 15, 1979 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
1100 Kay Street 
Sacramento, Calif. 95804 
Attention: Mr. Alan Herndon 
Compliance Representative 
Dear Mr. Herndon: 
I note from your FPPC Bulletin of May 11th that 
you have adopted auditing standards and guidelines affecting 
the financial review of campaign and lobbying statements. 
I would appreciate receiving a copy of the 
standards and guidelines which you adopted and will be 
pleased to send you whatever charge there is for it. 
If copies of the recommendations of Coopers & 
Lybrand are available, I would be grateful if you could send 
that to us as well. 
I would like to make a similar recom-
mendation for our Commission and would appreciate your views 
concerning the recommendations that were made and adopted. 
I would assume that the guidelines and the backup material 
relate to the regulation you refer to as 2 Cal. Adm. Code 
Section 18916. 
We are still grop~ng with many of our problems. 
As you may know, our law provides for a new chairman each 
year, to be elected by the commissioners, with each chair-
man to be of a different political faith than the predeces-
sor. 
I think it is plain rubbish to do so; confusing and 
devisive; tending to create bad feelings or bad blood and 
serves only one purpose - partisanship. 
I would welcome your news from time to time. 
SSK:m

sections.question_synthetic: The New York Commission on Regulation of Lobbying requests copies of California FPPC's newly adopted auditing standards and guidelines for financial review of campaign and lobbying statements, along with the Coopers & Lybrand recommendations, to inform similar recommendations for their own commission.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/correspondence document, not an FPPC advice letter response. No formal question or conclusion sections present. Document is a letter from an external state agency to the FPPC requesting information and materials. Contains some informal commentary about the requester's own state commission structure.

embedding.summary: An incoming letter from the New York State Commission on Regulation of Lobbying to the California FPPC requesting copies of their newly adopted auditing standards and guidelines for financial statement review, with interest in adopting similar standards for their own state commission.
